prevalence.value: 10-20% of hospitalized older adults; higher in ICU
prevalence.trend: stable; recognition increasing with screening tools

burden.dalyPerCapita: high per-case; linked to mortality and long-term cognitive decline

vulnerablePopulations: older adults with baseline cognitive impairment
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Acute change with fluctuating course and inattention; CAM or 4AT screening tools; identify and treat underlying cause

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; delirium screening is not universal
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Improves with prevention bundles and early recognition

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Screening tool use varies; often missed in hypoactive delirium
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Treat underlying cause, non-pharmacologic delirium bundles, avoid deliriogenic drugs; antipsychotics only for severe agitation when needed
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underrecognition and staffing constraints for prevention bundles
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Evidence-based prevention and management can be implemented with low-cost protocols
